Disease Management (Stem rot, Rust, Early leaf spot, Alternaria leaf blight)
Pest management (Gram pod borer)_Disease (Stem rot, Dry Root Rot, Rust, Early leaf spot)

advisory_content: Irrigation Pod Development Stage (60–80 Days After Sowing) During this stage, pods develop and kernels fill with nutrients. Moisture stress can lead to shriveled seeds and poor yields. Apply light but regular irrigations at 7–12 day intervals. Keep the field moist but not flooded to promote proper pod filling. Maintain weed-free conditions for better water use efficiency. Stop irrigation 10–12 days before harvest to allow pods to mature and dry properly.

Harvest the crop where plants turn yellow and leaves start drying original seed colour develop on the kernel and the pods develop blackish streak inside the shell. Bullock drawn groundnut digger should be used for uprooting. Groundnut stripper or power operated groundnut thresher can be used to remove the pod from the plants. At the time of harvesting pods usually have moisture content around 40-50% and hence need to be dried under direct sunlight to bring moisture content below 10% for safe storage. There are chances of fungal infection if the seed moisture content is above the critical level of 10%. Groundnut should be stored in polythene lined gunny bags with desiccant like fused Calcium Chloride @ 250-gram/30-kg of seeds. The desiccant should be tied with cotton cloth and kept inside perforated plastic box. The sealed bags should keep vertically inside a well-ventilated room at room temperature. This technique is very effective in maintaining high germination rate even after 8-10 months of storage.

Harvest the groundnut crop without delay at maturity. At the time of harvest, the moisture content of seeds is 40 to 50 percent. Therefore, after drying for a few days, the water content is kept at 10 percent. If the pods are stored with too much moisture content, they become damaged by fungi and produce less yield. Avoid heaping the product in the field itself. Store pods in airtight plastic bags to protect them from insect attack. In case of gunny bags, go for spraying of gunny bags with Spinosad 45% SC @ 5 ml/15 liters of water. Farmers can also keep Aluminum phosphide pouch in the warehouse where necessary. You can also add dry neem leaves, begonia leaves or edible oil to the groundnut bag. Cleanliness of the godown should be maintained.

Some developed varieties of green gram include TARM 1, TARM 2, Jyoti, Kamdev (OUM 11-5), Pusa Bold 1, Dhauli, Pragyan, Durga (OBGG-52), Shreya (IPM 02-14), Meha (IPM 99-125), LGG 460, TU 40, Shreejan.

Farmers are advised to start land preparation immediately after the paddy harvest especially when grown under residual soil moisture after the harvest. Plough the field 2–3 times to break clods and obtain a fine tilth. Level the land properly to conserve residual moisture and ensure uniform germination. In areas with poor drainage, make broad beds and furrows or raised beds to avoid waterlogging. Apply well-decomposed FYM or compost @ 2 t/acre during the last ploughing to improve soil fertility and moisture-holding capacity. In case of dry soil, apply one pre-sowing irrigation to ensure uniform germination. Avoid deep sowing and ensure proper seed-soil contact. If soil moisture is inadequate, delay sowing until light rainfall or irrigation is available. Farmers should ensure proper field bunding to retain moisture and prevent runoff. In areas prone to moisture stress, apply light irrigation at flowering and pod formation stages, which are critical for yield improvement. Protect seedlings from pests like cutworm, termites.

Treat 10 kg of seeds are required for sowing in an acre area. Seed treatment should be done before 1 week of sowing with carbendazim @ 2 g/ kg or Carboxin 37.5 % + Thiram 37.5% D.S @ 2 -gram/ kg of seeds. On the day of sowing prepare a slurry of 20 g bacteria culture with jaggery @ 50 gram and 40 ml water for 1 kg seed and mix it with seeds, then shade dry and sow it in afternoon. Apply Trichoderma viride @ 4g / kg of seed or Pseudomonas fluorescens @ 10 g/kg seed. To increase the biological nitrogen fixation mix Rhizobium 200 g and PSB 250 g in 600 ml water per 10 kg of seed followed by drying for 12 hrs. in shade. Seeds are treated with Thiamethoxam 30% FS @ 10 ml/ kg To prevent sucking pest and with carbosulfan 25 EC @ 2g/ Kg of seed to prevent nematode infestation. Sow the seeds at 20 cm x 10 cm spacing after seed treatment is done.
Plough the field till fine tilth and apply 4 kg urea, 35 kg DAP, 13 kg MOP along with 2 tons well decomposed cowdung manure. Sow the seeds at 25 c.m x 10 c.m spacing after seed treatment is done.
To manage weeds at early stages in these crops, spray pre-emergence herbicide Pendimethalin 30 % E.C @ 1 litre /acre at 1-2 DAS by mixing in 200-litre of water.

advisory_content: During the flowering stage (30–45 DAS), greengram is highly sensitive to moisture stress, so maintaining adequate soil moisture is crucial. If there is no rainfall for 7–10 days, provide light irrigation (4–5 cm depth) using the furrow or check basin method, avoiding water stagnation. Ensure proper drainage to prevent diseases like powdery mildew and rust. Irrigate in the early morning or late afternoon to reduce evaporation losses. Mulching with dry straw or crop residues helps conserve soil moisture. Avoid over-irrigation, as it may cause flower drop. For better pod setting and uniform flowering, apply a foliar spray of 2% DAP solution or NAA @ 40 ppm during early flowering.

advisory_content: During the pod formation stage (43–56 DAS), maintaining adequate soil moisture is vital for proper pod development and grain filling in greengram. If there is no rainfall for 7–10 days, provide light to moderate irrigation (4–5 cm depth) using the furrow or basin method, avoiding waterlogging. Mulching with crop residues or straw helps conserve moisture and regulate soil temperature. Irrigate in the early morning or late afternoon to reduce evaporation losses and ensure good drainage. Monitor for pests like pod borer, aphids, whitefly, and tobacco caterpillar, which are active during this stage. Timely application of neem-based or recommended insecticides helps protect pods while maintaining optimal soil moisture.

advisory_content: Harvest management and Storage practices in Greengram. Pods should be harvested at 70-80 % maturity when most of the pods turn black. Threshing is done manually or using machineries like vertical flow pulse thresher cum winnower and sun dried for 3 - 4 days to attain a moisture content of 8-10%. Apply edible oil @200 ml/quintal of grain or neem oil @200-250 ml / 50 kg of grain and mix it well to store for longer period. Mix neem leaves or begonia leaves to prevent storage pest attack @ 50g/kg of seeds.

After harvesting Greengram, dry the seeds until the optimum moisture content is attained. Clean the entire surface where the storage is to be done. Thereafter, mix 250 ml of any edible oil per quintal of seed to reduce the infestation of pest during the storage. Dried leaves of either Begonia or Neem @ 1 kg/Bag can also be kept inside the storage bag to avoid pest infestation. To decrease the pest infestation during storage, dip the gunny bags in boiling water for 15min then dry it properly under the sun before filling the seeds in the bags. Else prepare Malathion 5ml per litre of water solution and then dip the gunny bags in it for 15min and then dry it properly under the sun. Thereafter store the seeds in the bags.

Hybrid varieties like Sabita, Chandramukhi, Gabar, Akka, Bhagya, Bajrang, Dhano, Sri Tulasi, JK- Durga, Bunny. High Yielding Varieties like Suraj, MCU-5, Surabhi. A seed rate of 6-10 kg/acre in  case of American cottons and 4- 7 kg/acre in case of desi cotton.

After pre-monsoon rainfall go for summer ploughing. Plough the field with mould board plough or disc plough for deep ploughing. Due to overturning of the soil in summer ploughing the sharp rays of sun enters the soil and kills the eggs, larvae and pupae of soil borne insects and pests, there by the hazards of insects and pests on subsequent crop is reduced. Deep ploughing and overturning uproot the weeds and uproots the stubbles of previous crop. Consequently, the roots and stems of the weeds get desiccated and die. Keep the field clean. Apply 2 tonne of compost during last ploughing. Purchase suitable hybrid seeds and fertilizers from licenced dealer. Avoid growing longer duration hybrids in Rainfed area. In acidic soils (pH <6.0), incorporation of 100 kg gypsum/ acre during final land preparation is necessary for better crop performance. Apply Chlorpyriphos dust @ 10 kg per acre into the soil before sowing in termite affected fields.

Before sowing seed should be treated with Carbendazim 50% WP @ 2 gram, Carboxin 37.5% + Thiram 37.5% DS @ 3gram or Trichoderma viridae @ 4- 5 gram or Plantomycin @ 1 gram per kg of seed can protect the crop from fungal diseases such as root rot and seed rot. If seed is treated with Imidacloprid 70% WS @ 5-7 gram per kg of seed, the crop is not likely to be affected by sucking insects for 45-50 days.

Sowing in rows can be done either by dibbling , drilling or placing the seeds in furrows behind the country plough. Sowing in rows facilitates farmers in regulating seed rate, sowing depth, plant population etc. A spacing of 90cm between rows and 90cm between plants for American cottons and spacing of 90cm between rows and 60cm between plants for local cottons is desirable. Plant seeds 8-10 cm below the soil protects them from wilt disease. A seed rate of 6-10 kg/acre in  case of American cottons and 4- 7 kg/acre in case of desi cotton.

At the time of sowing apply 52 kg DAP, 21 Kg Urea and 20 kg Potash as basal fertilizer. Put some seeds in leaf pot or polythene at same time, when these seedlings are of 10 to 12 days old, gap filling can be done where the seeds are not germinated or replace the weaker plants. This will maintain proper plant population per unit area.

Spray pre-emergence herbicide Pendimethalin 30% EC @1-liter/acre by mixing in 200-liters of water at 0-3 DAS to control weeds at the early stage of crop. At the time of herbicide application sufficient soil moisture must be there. Always use clean water for herbicide application. For spraying of herbicide use flat fan nozzle or flood jet nozzle. Thanks.

advisory_content: At 20-25 DAS(Grass at its 3-4 leaf stage), to manage both grassy and broad leaf weeds in cotton spray Pyrithiobac sodium 6% EC W/W + Quizalofop-ethyl 4% EC W/W EC @ 450 ml/acre by mixing in 200-litre of water. In case of grassy weeds apply Quizalofop-ethyl @ 400 ml/acre by mixing with 200 lit water. Apply sticker @ 5 ml/15 lit with herbicide. Always use flat-fan or flood-jet nozzle and clean water for herbicide spraying. Thank You.

First top dressing of fertilizer should be done with 40 kg Urea and 15 kg MOP for high yielding varieties and for hybrids with 52 kg Urea and 20 kg MOP at 20-25 DAS. Regular monitoring of the crop for incidence of pest and diseases should be done. Provide adequate drainage channels to avoid water logging in the cotton fields. Thank You.

advisory_content: One hand pulling, hand weeding or inter culture with hand hoe or wheel hoe after 25-30 days after sowing and second if needed after 60 DAS. Thin out excess plants at 2 weeks stage. Follow hoeing, weeding, top dressing, and earthing up at 3-4 weeks and 6-7 weeks followed by irrigation. The apical portion of plant is removed when the plant will attain the height of 1m. to induce the formation of more reproductive branches is called topping. To prevent square and flower drop, spray NAA 4.5% SL (Planofix/Plantaid) @ 3.5 ml/15 litres of water at 45 DAS and 7-ml/15-litre at 60 DAS. When the tree reaches 3 feet in height or 90 DAP, pinching off the top of the tree will result in more fruit-bearing branches, more fruit, and increased yield. Provide adequate drainage channels to avoid water logging in the cotton fields.

advisory_content: At 55-60 DAS apply 20-kg of Urea for HYVs and 26-kg of Urea for Hybrids as final top-dressing fertilizer. Regular monitoring of the crop for incidence of pest and diseases should be done. Provide adequate drainage channels to avoid water logging in the cotton fields.

One hand pulling, hand weeding or inter culture with hand hoe or wheel hoe after 25-30 days after sowing and second if needed after 60 DAS. Thin out excess plants at 2 weeks stage. Follow hoeing, weeding, top dressing, and earthing up at 3-4 weeks and 6-7 weeks followed by irrigation. The apical portion of plant is removed when the plant will attain the height of 1m. to induce the formation of more reproductive branches is called topping. To prevent square and flower drop, spray NAA 4.5% SL (Planofix/Plantaid) @ 3.5 ml/15 litres of water at 45 DAS and 7-ml/15-litre at 60 DAS. When the tree reaches 3 feet in height or 90 DAP, pinching off the top of the tree will result in more fruit-bearing branches, more fruit, and increased yield. Provide adequate drainage channels to avoid water logging in the cotton fields.

advisory_code: OD-ALL-K-C-BF-First Picking and Crop Growth Management
advisory_title: First Picking and Crop Growth Management
advisory_content: Farmers are advised to go for picking cotton on sunny days after 9 AM or when dew dries up from the crop surface. Dry the seed Cotton on concrete floor or tarpaulin for 2-3 hours in direct sunlight and keep it in clean cloth bags. Spray Urea or Potassium Nitrate (KNO3) @ 2% (20 gram per 1 litre of water) after first picking for better development of the crop and remaining bolls.

The first pod starts to open in 110-120 days. Harvesting should be done at frequent intervals, at 10-15 days interval. Harvest When the bolls open well after 9 am only when there is moisture so that dry leaves and bracts do not stick to the kapas. Pick kapas from well burst bolls only. Remove only the kapas from the bolls and leave the bracts on the plants. After kapas is picked, sort out good puffy ones and keep separately. Keep stained, discoloured and insect attacked kapas separately.

kapas should be dried under sky after picking. Dry the kapas under direct sun 3 to 4 times as the fibre strength and luster will be lost. Dry the kapas in clean concrete surface or on a polythene sheet. Grade the kapas into good and second quality ones, if it is not sorted out at the time of picking. Spread a thin layer of dry sand on the ground and keep the kapas over it.

Some improved varieties of Arhar are PRG-235, PRG 176, VL ARHAR-1, T-Arhar, Asha, Laxman, Maruti etc. For line sowing the recommended seed rate for early maturing varieties is 8 kg per acre whereas for late maturing varieties it is 6 kg per acre. Land should be ploughed 3-4 times by cultivator to get fine tilth followed by land levelling. Apply 2 tonne of well decomposed FYM during final land preparation. Seed treatment can be done with Carbendazim 50 % WP @ 2 gram/ kg of seeds or Carboxin 37.5 % + Thiram 37.5% D.S WP @ 3 gram/ kg of seeds. Treat the seeds with recommended species of Rhizobium bacterial culture @ 20 gram/kg of seeds before 1 hour of sowing. The gap between chemical seed treatment and Rhizobium treatment should be at least 7 days.

Since the roots of arhar crops go deep into the soil, the land should be well cultivated. If the soil is well compacted, the seeds germinate easily and evenly. Therefore, the land should be ploughed 3-4 times with a plough to make the soil compact and level. The more compact the soil, the better. If the grass seeds are sown in rows, the work of weeding, weeding and grain storage can be done easily at a later stage. In sandy loam land, as soon as the soil is ripe, two ploughs are done in the same day, followed by sowing and fertilizing, which gives good growth. But in clay and sandy loam land, after repeated ploughing and fertilizing, the seeds should be sown and fertilized. In early Rabi and Rabi arhar crops, irrigation should be taken advantage of and the seeds should be sown in the appropriate weather. After sowing, irrigation is given to the seeds for good growth. If the seeds are sown using the soil, soak the seeds for 5-6 hours before sowing. Sow the seeds as follows according to the variety. Sow the seeds in row to row and plant to plant spacing should be 60 cm and 20 cm respectively. Sow the seeds in 3-5 cm depth.

For line sowing the recommended seed rate for early maturing varieties is 8 kg per acre whereas for late maturing varieties it is 6 kg per acre. The number of plants in the field depends on the germination capacity of the seeds. Therefore, the number of plants required in the field should be tested before sowing. Soak the seeds in water for 4 - 6 hours and tie them in a cloth or cover them with sand in a container. Germination occurs in a week. The required number of plants can be obtained if at least 80 percent of the seeds germinate. To protect the crop from root rot, leaf blight, seed treatment, and powdery mildew, etc. Seed treatment should be done. Seed treatment can be done with Carbendazim 50 % WP @ 2 gram/ kg of seeds or Carboxin 37.5 % + Thiram 37.5% D.S WP @ 3 gram/ kg of seeds. Treat the seeds with recommended species of Rhizobium bacterial culture @ 20 gram/kg of seeds before 1 hour of sowing. The gap between chemical seed treatment and Rhizobium treatment should be at least 7 days. For bacterial culture treatment, keep seeds on a clean floor or polythene. Then mix 1 kg of the specified bacterial culture (Rhizobium) with 2 liters of water to prepare a liquid slurry. Pour this liquid slurry gently on the seeds and mix it with your hands so that a coating is formed around each seed. Remember, treat the seeds a week before mixing the bacterial culture. Dry the treated seeds in the shade and sow them in the field.

Today we will discuss about Basal Fertilizer application in Arhar cultivation. For Arhar seeds the row to row and plant to plant spacing should be 60 cm and 20 cm respectively. Sow the seeds in 3-5 cm depth. Apply 35 kg DAP, 4 kg Urea and 13 kg MOP as basal fertilizer per acre. Apply nano urea @ 4ml/lit of water at 20-25 days after sowing.

Today we will discuss about Preemergence weed management in Arhar. To manage weeds at early stages in these crops, spray pre-emergence herbicide Pendimethalin 30 % EC @ 1 litre /acre at 1-2 DAS by mixing in 200 litre of water followed by hand weeding at 20 days after sowing.

advisory_code: OD-ALL-K-A-GS-Pest and disease (Stem fly, Dry root rot, Fusarium Wilt, Yellow Vein Mosaic)
advisory_title: Pest and disease (Stem fly, Dry root rot, Fusarium Wilt, Yellow Vein Mosaic)

advisory_content: Today we will discuss about weed management in Arhar. If pre-emergence herbicide is not applied during sowing than to manage weeds in these crops post emergence application of herbicide like Imazethapyr 10 % SL (For both narrow and broadleaf weeds) @ 300-ml/acre or Quizalofop-Ethyl 5 % EC (Only for narrow weeds) @ 400-ml/acre should be applied at 15-DAS by mixing it in 200 litres of water controls the grassy weeds effectively. Always use flat-fan or flood-jet nozzle and clean water for herbicide spraying.

advisory_content: During rainy season, the Arhar crop does not require water. If there is no rain during flowering, then it is necessary to water it. Apart from this, the Arhar crop needs to be irrigated. In terms of irrigation, the critical period of the crop is the flowering stage and grain filling. If there is water in the field during rainy season, the plant does not grow well. Therefore, it is essential to make good arrangements for drainage.

advisory_content: Today we will discuss about the harvest and storage management in red gram crop. Harvest the red gram crop when 70-80 % Pods turn brown. Over maturity can cause shattering of seeds which results in lower yield. Harvesting should be done manually by using a sickle. Harvested plants should be kept in threshing floor for sun drying for 3-6 days depending on season. After that threshing should be done by beating the pods with stick. Clean harvested seeds should be dried under direct sunlight to bring the moisture content below 10-12% for safe storage. Dal processing should be done in composite mini dal mill. To avoid storage pests’ infestation during storage smearing of seeds should be done with edible oil @ 250 ml/ Quintal of seeds. Dried leaves of either Begonia or Neem @ 1 kg/Bag should be kept inside the storage bag to minimize storage pest load. Camphor tablets @ 1-tablet/kg of seeds can also be kept inside the storage bag to avoid storage pests. Thank You.

After harvesting Arhar, dry the seeds until the optimum moisture content is attained. Clean the entire surface where the storage is to be done. Thereafter, mix 250 ml of any edible oil per quintal of seed to reduce the infestation of pest during the storage. Dried leaves of either Begonia or Neem @ 1 kg/Bag can also be kept inside the storage bag to avoid pest infestation. To decrease the pest infestation during storage, dip the gunny bags in boiling water for 15min then dry it properly under the sun before filling the seeds in the bags. Else prepare Malathion 5ml per litre of water solution and then dip the gunny bags in it for 15min and then dry it properly under the sun. Thereafter store the seeds in the bags.
